title: Winey Velvety Brazilian Chicken Breast Paella
description: A romantic and flavorful Brazilian dish featuring chicken breast, ferment-jang, and a wine-infused velvety sauce, all in one pan. Serve with a side of steaming hot rice for a truly unforgettable meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan over medium heat.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the white wine and bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Add the chicken broth, ferment-jang, red bell pepper, yellow corn, saffron, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 15 minutes.
While the paella is cooking, make the velvety sauce by heating the heavy cream in a small saucepan over medium heat until it starts to simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the cream for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
To serve, place the paella on a large platter and spoon the velvety sauce over the top.
Garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Serve hot, with a side of steaming hot rice.
Let the paella rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
